lines.gurmukhi: ਸੂਹੈ ਵੇਸਿ ਪਿਰੁ ਕਿਨੈ ਨ ਪਾਇਓ ਮਨਮੁਖਿ ਦਝਿ ਮੁਈ ਗਾਵਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: soohai ves pir kinai na paaeo manamukh dajh muee gaavaar |
lines.english: By wearing her red dress, no one has found her Husband Lord; the self-willed manmukh is burnt to death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨੁ ਤਨੁ ਰਤਾ ਲਾਲੁ ਹੋਆ ਰਸਨਾ ਰਤੀ ਗੁਣ ਸਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: man tan rataa laal hoaa rasanaa ratee gun saar |
lines.english: Her mind and body are imbued with the deep red color of His Love, and her tongue is imbued, singing His Praises and excellences.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਸੂਹਾ ਵੇਸੁ ਗਇਆ ਹਉਮੈ ਵਿਚਹੁ ਮਾਰਿ ॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai soohaa ves geaa haumai vichahu maar |
lines.english: Meeting the True Guru, she discards her red dress, and eradicates egotism from within.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕਰਮੀ ਮਹਲੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਪਿਰੁ ਰਾਖਿਆ ਉਰ ਧਾਰਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak karamee mehal paaeaa pir raakhiaa ur dhaar |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, by His Merciful Grace, she obtains the Mansion of the Lord's Presence, and keeps Him enshrined in her heart. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਦਾ ਸੋਹਾਗਣਿ ਸਬਦੁ ਮਨਿ ਭੈ ਭਾਇ ਕਰੇ ਸੀਗਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: sadaa sohaagan sabad man bhai bhaae kare seegaar |
lines.english: She becomes His soul-bride forever, with the Word of the Shabad in her mind; she makes the Fear of God and the Love of God her ornaments and decorations.

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁੰਧੇ ਸੂਹਾ ਪਰਹਰਹੁ ਲਾਲੁ ਕਰਹੁ ਸੀਗਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: mundhe soohaa paraharahu laal karahu seegaar |
lines.english: O bride, forsake your red dress, and decorate yourself with the crimson color of His Love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਵਣ ਜਾਣਾ ਵੀਸਰੈ ਗੁਰਸਬਦੀ ਵੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: aavan jaanaa veesarai gurasabadee veechaar |
lines.english: Your comings and goings shall be forgotten, contemplating the Word of the Guru's Shabad.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੁੰਧ ਸੁਹਾਵੀ ਸੋਹਣੀ ਜਿਸੁ ਘਰਿ ਸਹਜਿ ਭਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: mundh suhaavee sohanee jis ghar sehaj bhataar |
lines.english: The soul-bride is adorned and beautiful; the Celestial Lord, her Husband, abides in her home.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਾ ਧਨ ਰਾਵੀਐ ਰਾਵੇ ਰਾਵਣਹਾਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak saa dhan raaveeai raave raavanahaar |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the bride ravishes and enjoys Him; and He, the Ravisher, ravishes and enjoys her. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਉਮੈ ਮੇਰਾ ਕਰਿ ਮੁਏ ਕਿਛੁ ਸਾਥਿ ਨ ਲਿਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: haumai meraa kar mue kichh saath na litaa |
lines.english: Practicing egotism and self-conceit, he dies and departs, taking nothing along with him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਰ ਉਪਰਿ ਜਮਕਾਲੁ ਨ ਸੁਝਈ ਦੂਜੈ ਭਰਮਿਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: sir upar jamakaal na sujhee doojai bharamitaa |
lines.english: He does not understand that the Messenger of Death is hovering over his head; he is deluded by duality.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੋਹੁ ਕੂੜੁ ਕੁਟੰਬੁ ਹੈ ਮਨਮੁਖੁ ਮੁਗਧੁ ਰਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: mohu koorr kuttanb hai manamukh mugadh rataa |
lines.english: The foolish, self-willed manmukh is engrossed in false attachment to family.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇਹਾ ਧੁਰਿ ਲਿਖਿ ਪਾਇਓਨੁ ਸੇ ਕਰਮ ਕਮਿਤਾ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: jehaa dhur likh paaeon se karam kamitaa |5|
lines.english: He acts according to his pre-ordained destiny. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਫਿਰਿ ਵੇਲਾ ਹਥਿ ਨ ਆਵਈ ਜਮਕਾਲਿ ਵਸਿ ਕਿਤਾ ॥
lines.translit: fir velaa hath na aavee jamakaal vas kitaa |
lines.english: This opportunity will not come into his hands again; the Messenger of Death will seize him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤੀਆ ਏਹਿ ਨ ਆਖੀਅਨਿ ਜੋ ਮੜਿਆ ਲਗਿ ਜਲੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: sateea ehi na aakheean jo marriaa lag jalana |
lines.english: Do not call them 'satee', who burn themselves along with their husbands' corpses.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੩ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Salok, Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਤੀਆ ਜਾਣੀਅਨੑਿ ਜਿ ਬਿਰਹੇ ਚੋਟ ਮਰੰਨੑਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak sateea jaaneeana ji birahe chott marana |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, they alone are known as 'satee', who die from the shock of separation. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਭੀ ਸੋ ਸਤੀਆ ਜਾਣੀਅਨਿ ਸੀਲ ਸੰਤੋਖਿ ਰਹੰਨੑਿ ॥
lines.translit: bhee so sateea jaaneean seel santokh rahana |
lines.english: They are also known as 'satee', who abide in modesty and contentment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੰਤਾ ਨਾਲਿ ਮਹੇਲੀਆ ਸੇਤੀ ਅਗਿ ਜਲਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: kantaa naal maheleea setee ag jalaeh |
lines.english: The widows burn themselves in the fire, along with their husbands' corpses.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਵਨਿ ਸਾਈ ਆਪਣਾ ਨਿਤ ਉਠਿ ਸੰਮੑਾਲੰਨੑਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: sevan saaee aapanaa nit utth samaalana |2|
lines.english: They serve their Lord, and rise in the early hours to contemplate Him. ||2||

lines.translit: mahalaa 3 |
lines.english: Third Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਕੰਤ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੀ ਸੇ ਕਿਉ ਅਗਿ ਜਲਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: naanak kant na jaananee se kiau ag jalaeh |
lines.english: O Nanak, if they did not truly know their husbands, why should they burn themselves in the fire?

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਜਾਣਹਿ ਪਿਰੁ ਆਪਣਾ ਤਾ ਤਨਿ ਦੁਖ ਸਹਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: je jaaneh pir aapanaa taa tan dukh sahaeh |
lines.english: If they truly knew their husbands, then they suffer terrible bodily pain.

lines.gurmukhi: ਭਾਵੈ ਜੀਵਉ ਕੈ ਮਰਉ ਦੂਰਹੁ ਹੀ ਭਜਿ ਜਾਹਿ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: bhaavai jeevau kai mrau doorahu hee bhaj jaeh |3|
lines.english: Whether their husbands are alive or dead, those wives remain far away from them. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੁਧੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਸੁਖੁ ਨਾਲਿ ਉਪਾਇਆ ਲੇਖੁ ਕਰਤੈ ਲਿਖਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: tudh dukh sukh naal upaaeaa lekh karatai likhiaa |
lines.english: You created pain along with pleasure; O Creator, such is the writ You have written.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਵੈ ਜੇਵਡ ਹੋਰ ਦਾਤਿ ਨਾਹੀ ਤਿਸੁ ਰੂਪੁ ਨ ਰਿਖਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: naavai jevadd hor daat naahee tis roop na rikhiaa |
lines.english: There is no other gift as great as the Name; it has no form or sign.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੁ ਅਖੁਟੁ ਨਿਧਾਨੁ ਹੈ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਮਨਿ ਵਸਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: naam akhutt nidhaan hai guramukh man vasiaa |
lines.english: The Naam, the Name of the Lord, is an inexhaustible treasure; it abides in the mind of the Gurmukh.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਵਕ ਭਾਇ ਸੇ ਜਨ ਮਿਲੇ ਜਿਨ ਹਰਿ ਜਪੁ ਜਪਿਆ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: sevak bhaae se jan mile jin har jap japiaa |6|
lines.english: Those humble servants who serve with love, meet the Lord, chanting the Chant of the Lord. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਿ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਨਾਮੁ ਦੇਵਸੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਲੇਖੁ ਨ ਲਿਖਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: kar kirapaa naam devasee fir lekh na likhiaa |
lines.english: In His Mercy, He blesses us with the Naam, and then, the writ of pain and pleasure is not written.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨੀ ਚਲਣੁ ਜਾਣਿਆ ਸੇ ਕਿਉ ਕਰਹਿ ਵਿਥਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: jinee chalan jaaniaa se kiau kareh vithaar |
lines.english: They know that they will have to depart, so why do they make such ostentatious displays?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕੁ ਮਃ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਲਣ ਸਾਰ ਨ ਜਾਣਨੀ ਕਾਜ ਸਵਾਰਣਹਾਰ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: chalan saar na jaananee kaaj savaaranahaar |1|
lines.english: Those who do not know that they will have to depart, continue to arrange their affairs. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਤਿ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਧਨੁ ਸੰਚੀਐ ਭਲਕੇ ਚਲਣੁ ਹੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: raat kaaran dhan sancheeai bhalake chalan hoe |
lines.english: He accumulates wealth during the night of his life, but in the morning, he must depart.

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਲਿ ਨ ਚਲਈ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਛੁਤਾਵਾ ਹੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak naal na chalee fir pachhutaavaa hoe |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, it shall not go along with him, and so he regrets. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੇਤੀ ਖੁਸੀ ਸਵਾਰੀਐ ਨਾਨਕ ਕਾਰਜੁ ਸਾਰੁ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: setee khusee savaareeai naanak kaaraj saar |3|
lines.english: That alone is a good deed, O Nanak, which is done by one's own free will. ||3||

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਧਾ ਚਟੀ ਜੋ ਭਰੇ ਨਾ ਗੁਣੁ ਨਾ ਉਪਕਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: badhaa chattee jo bhare naa gun naa upakaar |
lines.english: Paying a fine under pressure, does not bring either merit or goodness.

lines.translit: mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਰਫ ਜਿਣੈ ਸਤ ਭਾਉ ਦੇ ਜਨ ਨਾਨਕ ਸਬਦੁ ਵੀਚਾਰੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: taraf jinai sat bhaau de jan naanak sabad veechaare |4|
lines.english: The Lord is won over to your side, by offering Him your true love, O servant Nanak, and contemplating the Word of the Shabad. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਹਠਿ ਤਰਫ ਨ ਜਿਪਈ ਜੇ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਘਾਲੇ ॥
lines.translit: manahatth taraf na jipee je bahutaa ghaale |
lines.english: Stubborn-mindedness will not win the Lord to one's side, no matter how much it is tried.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਰਤੈ ਕਾਰਣੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਕੀਆ ਸੋ ਜਾਣੈ ਸੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: karatai kaaran jin keea so jaanai soee |
lines.english: The Creator created the world; He alone understands it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਪੇ ਸ੍ਰਿਸਟਿ ਉਪਾਈਅਨੁ ਆਪੇ ਫੁਨਿ ਗੋਈ ॥
lines.translit: aape srisatt upaaeean aape fun goee |
lines.english: He Himself created the Universe, and He Himself shall destroy it afterwards.
